mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in income and housing starts fail to predict negative returns with a rank IC above 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in the industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts short-term corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth correlates with negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of observations during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 208 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns are positive in > 70% of months where CPI exceeds the 208 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising aggregate wages, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10) across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and real personal income confirms broad-based demand growth that translates into higher corporate earnings forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model with an information coefficient below 0.02 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of financial stress, a contraction in industrial production signals supply chain disruptions that immediately penalize equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production growth fails to predict negative 5-day returns with a win rate above 55% during identified stress regimes.
